row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of $716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B USD. The top two companies hold a significant revenue advantage over the third-ranked UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) with a $269.4B USD gap.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of $132.2B USD, outpacing NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by $12.1B USD. The top 3 companies (GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) account for 63.9% of the total Net Income among the top 5.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with a massive $4.4T in Total Assets, dwarfing its closest competitor Bank of America Corporation (BAC) by $1T. The gap between JPM and the next closest company is staggering, with JPM's assets exceeding the combined totals of AMZN, MSFT, and GOOGL.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with $415.3B in Total Equity, a $63.8B advantage over Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) in second place.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, a significant gap from the second-largest, Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with 3.1T USD in liabilities. This 30% gap underscores the substantial financial scale of JPM.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the top companies with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the ranking with a Gross Profit of $360.5B USD, outpacing Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) by $120.2B USD. The gap between AMZN and the second-place GOOGL is significant, representing a 60% difference in Gross Profit.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL leads the pack with a staggering $133.1B Operating Income Ebit, outpacing its closest rival NVDA by $3.7B. The top 4 companies collectively generate $375.3B in Operating Income Ebit, with a notable gap between AAPL and the rest of the group.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate revenue generation, accounting for 43.1% of the total, with Consumer Cyclical at 22.0% and Technology at 21.1%. Consumer Cyclical is the largest sector, but Technology is a close second.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets with 64.1% share, significantly outpacing other sectors. This concentration in Financial Services may indicate a high reliance on the sector's performance for overall market stability.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with 39.2% share, outpacing Communication Services by 14.4 percentage points. The top two sectors, Technology and Communication Services, account for 64% of total net income.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin is significantly higher than UNH's 2.7%, indicating a substantial difference in profitability efficiency between the two companies. This disparity highlights NVDA's ability to effectively convert revenue into net income.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional return on equity (ROE) of 193.5%, while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) exhibits high leverage with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x, indicating a potential risk-reward tradeoff.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL led the pack with a 101.7% return, outpacing the market significantly. Conversely, NFLX suffered the largest decline at -41.3%, a stark contrast to the tech giant's success. This dichotomy highlights the varying fortunes of high-revenue companies in the past year.
